decisions.title: Adopt Supabase as the primary backend platform
decisions.context: We needed a backend platform covering auth, database, storage, and realtime. Evaluated Firebase, PlanetScale with custom auth, and Supabase.
decisions.decision: Use Supabase. PostgreSQL underneath gives full SQL portability, RLS enforces security at the database layer, and Lovable integrates natively without additional backend plumbing.
alternatives_rejected: Firebase — document model doesn't fit relational data, no SQL, significant vendor lock-in
PlanetScale + custom auth — too much infrastructure overhead for current team size
consequences: All database standards are anchored to PostgreSQL conventions — STD000000001 through STD000000006
RLS replaces a custom auth middleware layer, reducing backend surface area
Supabase Edge Function cold start latency is an accepted tradeoff for reduced ops burden
Supabase-specific RLS syntax is not portable if we migrate databases in future

decisions.title: Activity logging deferred to job completion for bulk-import feature
decisions.context: STD000000007 requires an activity log entry per write action within the same transaction. Bulk imports can process tens of thousands of rows in a single job.
decisions.decision: Write a single 'import.completed' activity log entry when the job finishes, recording the row count and source reference, rather than one entry per row.
alternatives_rejected: One log entry per row — prohibitively expensive at scale, causes transaction timeouts above ~5000 rows
Batch log entries in chunks — adds complexity with no meaningful audit benefit over a single completion entry
consequences: Individual row-level changes within a bulk import are not attributable in the activity log
Import jobs must always write the completion entry on success and a failure entry on error — partial imports must not be silently swallowed

exception_to.scope: bulk-import feature only — activity log entry written on job completion, not per-row
